title: Design Evaluation and Optimization in Discrete Space
text: example based landmark PK/PD population study Tolmetin (non-steroidal anti-inflammatory drug) rats (Flores-Murrieta et al. 1998). integrated model couples one-compartment PK model indirect response PD model. Original experimental design. Six parallel groups rats (n≥6n \geq 6 per group) received single oral doses 1 100 mg/kg. Blood samples inflammation scores (DI) collected 10 time points: 0, 0.25, 0.5, 0.75, 1, 1.25, 1.5, 2, 3, 4 h. Objectives: Evaluation – Compute Population Bayesian Fisher Information Matrices (FIM) original design. Assess parameter precision via RSE% Shrinkage. Optimization – Find D-optimal design 30 rats selecting doses sampling times discrete candidate set. Two algorithms compared: Fedorov-Wynn Multiplicative.

what: PK model: one-compartment, first-order oral absorption
title: Design Evaluation and Optimization in Discrete Space
text: dCcdt=DVkae−kat−ClVCc\frac{dC_c}{dt} = \frac{D}{V}\,k_a\,e^{-k_a t} - \frac{Cl}{V}\,C_c

what: PD model: indirect response, inhibition of production
title: Design Evaluation and Optimization in Discrete Space
text: dEdt=Rin(1−ImaxCcγCcγ+IC50γ)−koutE\frac{dE}{dt} = R_{}\!\left(1 - \frac{I_{max}\,C_c^{\gamma}}{C_c^{\gamma} + IC_{50}^{\gamma}}\right) - k_{}\,E Steady-state initial condition. t=0t = 0 (.e. Cc=0C_c = 0), PD system equilibrium: E(0)=Rin/kout=614/6.14=100E(0) = R_{}/k_{} = 614/6.14 = 100. Naming conventions ODE models: prefix Deriv_ mandatory; suffix must match state variable name (Cc E). dose specified dose_ followed name PK response (e.g. dose_RespPK). Use ** exponentiation. Model outcomes represented list associating response state variable: RespPK ↔\leftrightarrowCc, RespPD ↔\leftrightarrowE.
code: modelEquations = list(   Deriv_Cc = "dose_RespPK/V*ka*exp(-ka*t) - Cl/V*Cc",   Deriv_E  = "Rin*(1-Imax*(Cc**gamma)/(Cc**gamma + IC50**gamma)) - kout*E" )

title: Design Evaluation and Optimization in Discrete Space
text: Parameters specified via population typical value (fixed effect μ\mu) inter-individual variability (IIV ω\omega). PFIM assumes log-normal distribution parameters. estimable parameter vector dimension p=12p = 12 (6 fixed effects + 6 variance components): θ={μV,μCl,μkout,μImax,μIC50,μγ,ωV2,ωCl2,ωkout2,ωImax2,ωIC502,ωγ2}\theta = \bigl\{\mu_V,\,\mu_{Cl},\,\mu_{k_{}},\,\mu_{I_{max}},\, \mu_{IC_{50}},\,\mu_{\gamma},\; \omega^2_V,\,\omega^2_{Cl},\,\omega^2_{k_{}},\,\omega^2_{I_{max}},\, \omega^2_{IC_{50}},\,\omega^2_{\gamma}\bigr\} Setting fixedMu = TRUE excludes fixed effect FIM. Setting omega = 0 implies IIV; corresponding variance estimated (fixedOmega = TRUE set automatically).
code: modelParameters = list(   ModelParameter(name         = "V",                  distribution = LogNormal(mu = 0.74,  omega = 0.316)),   ModelParameter(name         = "Cl",                  distribution = LogNormal(mu = 0.28,  omega = 0.456)),   ModelParameter(name         = "ka",                  distribution = LogNormal(mu = 10,    omega = 0),                  fixedMu      = TRUE),   ModelParameter(name         = "kout",                  distribution = LogNormal(mu = 6.14,  omega = 0.947)),   ModelParameter(name         = "Rin",                  distribution = LogNormal(mu = 614,   omega = 0),                  fixedMu      = TRUE),   ModelParameter(name         = "Imax",                  distribution = LogNormal(mu = 0.76,  omega = 0.439)),   ModelParameter(name         = "IC50",                  distribution = LogNormal(mu = 9.22,  omega = 0.452)),   ModelParameter(name         = "gamma",                  distribution = LogNormal(mu = 2.77,  omega = 1.761)) )

title: Design Evaluation and Optimization in Discrete Space
text: Objective. Find D-optimal design future study practical constraints: Total sample size fixed 30 subjects single constrained arm. Doses restricted discrete set: {0.2, 0.64, 2, 6.32, 11.24, 20} mg. RespPK: 2 mandatory times + 4 optimisable times (7 candidates). RespPD: 2 mandatory times + 4 optimisable times (8 candidates).

title: Design Evaluation and Optimization in Discrete Space
code: #  Initial administration # Starting dose; optimizer will reassign from the discrete set in Section 2.6. adminRespPK = Administration(outcome = "RespPK", timeDose = 0, dose = 6.32)  # Candidate sampling grids sampTimesOptPK = SamplingTimes( outcome = "RespPK", samplings = c(0.25, 0.75, 1, 1.5, 2, 4, 6) ) sampTimesOptPD = SamplingTimes( outcome   = "RespPD", samplings = c(0.25, 0.75, 1.5, 2, 3, 6, 8, 12) )  # Sampling constraints -- RespPK # Fixed:       0.25 h (absorption/Cmax) and 4 h (late elimination). # Optimisable: 4 times chosen from {0.75, 1, 1.5, 2, 6}. sampConstraintsPK = SamplingTimeConstraints(   outcome                      = "RespPK",   initialSamplings             = c(0.25, 0.75, 1, 1.5, 2, 4, 6),   fixedTimes                   = c(0.25, 4),   numberOfsamplingsOptimisable = 4 )  # Sampling constraints -- RespPD # Fixed:       2 h (near peak effect / IC50) and 6 h (mid-recovery / kout). # Optimisable: 4 times chosen from {0.25, 0.75, 1.5, 3, 8, 12}. sampConstraintsPD = SamplingTimeConstraints(   outcome                      = "RespPD",   initialSamplings             = c(0.25, 0.75, 1.5, 2, 3, 6, 8, 12),   fixedTimes                   = c(2, 6),   numberOfsamplingsOptimisable = 4 )  # 2.5 Initial elementary protocols (Fedorov-Wynn only) initialElementaryProtocols = list(   c(0.25, 0.75, 1, 4),  # PK-focused: absorption + elimination   c(1.5,  2, 6, 12)     # PD-focused: near-peak + late recovery )  # Dose constraints -- discrete set matching the original study adminConstraintsPK = AdministrationConstraints(   outcome = "RespPK",   doses   = list(0.2, 0.64, 2, 6.32, 11.24, 20) )  # Constrained arm and design # E(0) = "Rin/kout" as a formula string: PFIM evaluates this at typical values, # giving E0 = 614/6.14 = 100. This is preferable to hardcoding the numeric # value because it stays consistent if parameter estimates are updated. armConstraint = Arm(   name                       = "armConstraint",   size                       = 30,   administrations            = list(adminRespPK),   samplingTimes              = list(sampTimesOptPK, sampTimesOptPD),   administrationsConstraints = list(adminConstraintsPK),   samplingTimesConstraints   = list(sampConstraintsPK, sampConstraintsPD),   initialCondition           = list(Cc = 0, E = "Rin/kout") )  # numberOfArms: upper bound on distinct elementary protocols designConstraint = Design(   name         = "designConstraints",   arms         = list(armConstraint),   numberOfArms = 30 )  numberOfSubjects      = 30 proportionsOfSubjects = 1   # single group at init; optimizer redistributes

what: Model Equations from from Library of Models
title: Design Evaluation and Optimization in Continuous Space
text: PFIM provides library pre-implemented standard PK/PD structural models. modelFromLibrary replaces modelEquations — two arguments mutually exclusive. key "PKModel" used pharmacokinetic models. string "Linear1InfusionSingleDose_ClV" decodes : predicted concentration follows: Cc(t)={DTinf⋅Cl(1−e−ClVt)0≤t≤TinfCc(Tinf)e−ClV(t−Tinf)t>TinfC_c(t) = \begin{cases} \dfrac{D}{T_{inf} \cdot Cl}\!\left(1 - e^{-\frac{Cl}{V}t}\right) & 0 \leq t \leq T_{inf} \\[6pt] C_c(T_{inf})\,e^{-\frac{Cl}{V}(t - T_{inf})} & t > T_{inf} \end{cases}
code: modelFromLibrary = list("PKModel" = "Linear1InfusionSingleDose_ClV")

title: Design Evaluation and Optimization in Continuous Space
text: model two structural parameters, log-normally distributed: IIV ~51% VV reflects moderate variability body composition; ~58% ClCl typical metabolic clearance variability. Note ω\omega standard deviation log scale: omega = sqrt(variance).
code: modelParameters = list(   ModelParameter(name = "V",  distribution = LogNormal(mu = 50, omega = sqrt(0.26))),   ModelParameter(name = "Cl", distribution = LogNormal(mu = 5,  omega = sqrt(0.34))) )

title: Design Evaluation and Optimization in Continuous Space
text: Combined1 error model used PK response.
code: errorModelRespPK = Combined1( output = "RespPK", sigmaInter = 0.5, sigmaSlope = sqrt( 0.15 ) ) modelError = list( errorModelRespPK )

title: Design Evaluation and Optimization in Continuous Space
text: dosing schedule encodes full multiple-dose regimen. loading dose (400 mg = 2× maintenance) rapidly raises CcC_c toward therapeutic target; subsequent 200 mg doses maintain near-steady-state concentrations.
code: administrationRespPK = Administration(   outcome  = "RespPK",   Tinf     = rep(1, 5),           # 1-hour infusion for each of the 5 doses   timeDose = seq(0, 96, 24),      # dosing at t = 0, 24, 48, 72, 96 h   dose     = c(400, rep(200, 4))  # 400 mg loading + 4 × 200 mg maintenance )

code: # Note: The 'initialConditions' slot is strictly used for ODE-based model arms. # For analytic (closed-form) solutions, this slot is ignored as the # initial state is implicitly defined by the model equations.  # 1. Define sampling times for PK and PD outcomes samplingTimesRespPK = SamplingTimes(outcome = "RespPK",                                      samplings = c(0.25, 0.5, 0.75, 1, 1.25, 1.5, 2, 3, 4))  samplingTimesRespPD = SamplingTimes(outcome = "RespPD",                                      samplings = c(0.25, 0.5, 0.75, 1, 1.25, 1.5, 2, 3, 4))  # 2. Define the administration (Dose of 20 at t=0) adminRespPK = Administration(outcome = "RespPK", timeDose = 0, dose = 20)  # 3. Define the study arm "0.2mg" # Outcomes are linked to state variables: RespPK to Cc, RespPD to E. arm = Arm(name = "0.2mg",            size = 6,            administrations = list(adminRespPK),            samplingTimes   = list(samplingTimesRespPK, samplingTimesRespPD),            initialConditions = list("Cc" = 0, "E" = 100))  print(arm) #> <PFIM::Arm> #>  @ name                      : chr "0.2mg" #>  @ size                      : num 6 #>  @ administrations           :List of 1 #>  .. $ : <PFIM::Administration> #>  ..  ..@ outcome : chr "RespPK" #>  ..  ..@ timeDose: num 0 #>  ..  ..@ dose    : num 20 #>  ..  ..@ Tinf    : num(0)  #>  ..  ..@ tau     : num 0 #>  @ initialConditions         :List of 2 #>  .. $ Cc: num 0 #>  .. $ E : num 100 #>  @ samplingTimes             :List of 2 #>  .. $ : <PFIM::SamplingTimes> #>  ..  ..@ outcome  : chr "RespPK" #>  ..  ..@ samplings: num [1:9] 0.25 0.5 0.75 1 1.25 1.5 2 3 4 #>  .. $ : <PFIM::SamplingTimes> #>  ..  ..@ outcome  : chr "RespPD" #>  ..  ..@ samplings: num [1:9] 0.25 0.5 0.75 1 1.25 1.5 2 3 4 #>  @ administrationsConstraints: list() #>  @ samplingTimesConstraints  : list() #>  @ evaluationModel           : list() #>  @ evaluationGradients       : list() #>  @ evaluationVariance        : list() #>  @ evaluationFim             : <PFIM::Fim> #>  .. @ fisherMatrix             : num(0)  #>  .. @ fixedEffects             : num(0)  #>  .. @ varianceEffects          : num(0)  #>  .. @ SEAndRSE                 : list() #>  .. @ condNumberFixedEffects   : num 0 #>  .. @ condNumberVarianceEffects: num 0 #>  .. @ shrinkage                : num(0)

code: # 1. Define sampling times for PK and PD outcomes samplingTimesRespPK = SamplingTimes(outcome   = "RespPK",                                      samplings = c(0.25, 0.5, 0.75, 1, 1.25, 1.5, 2, 3, 4))  samplingTimesRespPD = SamplingTimes(outcome   = "RespPD",                                      samplings = c(0.25, 0.5, 0.75, 1, 1.25, 1.5, 2, 3, 4))  # 2. Define the administration (Dose of 20 at t=0) adminRespPK = Administration(outcome = "RespPK", timeDose = 0, dose = 20)  # 3. Define the study arm "0.2mg" # Outcomes are linked to state variables: RespPK to Cc, RespPD to E. arm02mg = Arm(name = "0.2mg",                size = 6,                administrations   = list(adminRespPK),                samplingTimes     = list(samplingTimesRespPK, samplingTimesRespPD),                initialConditions = list("Cc" = 0, "E" = 100))  # 4. Create the Design object # The arm defined above is included in the 'arms' list. design1 = Design(name = "Design1",                   arms = list(arm02mg))  # Display the design summary print(design1) title: Fisher Information matrix for design evaluation/optimization for nonlinear mixed effects models. — PFIM-package
text: Evaluate optimize designs nonlinear mixed effects models using Fisher Information matrix. Methods used package refer Mentré F, Mallet , Baccar D (1997) doi:10.1093/biomet/84.2.429 , Retout S, Comets E, Samson , Mentré F (2007) doi:10.1002/sim.2910 , Bazzoli C, Retout S, Mentré F (2009) doi:10.1002/sim.3573 , Le Nagard H, Chao L, Tenaillon O (2011) doi:10.1186/1471-2148-11-326 , Combes FP, Retout S, Frey N, Mentré F (2013) doi:10.1007/s11095-013-1079-3  Seurat J, Tang Y, Mentré F, Nguyen TT (2021) doi:10.1016/j.cmpb.2021.106126 .

title: Fisher Information matrix for design evaluation/optimization for nonlinear mixed effects models. — PFIM-package
text: Nonlinear mixed effects models (NLMEM) widely used model-based drug development use analyze longitudinal data. use "population" Fisher Information Matrix (FIM) good alternative clinical trial simulation optimize design studies. present version, **PFIM 7.0**, R package uses S4 object system evaluating /optimizing population designs based FIM NLMEMs. version **PFIM** now includes library models implemented also using object oriented system S4 R. library contains two libraries pharmacokinetic (PK) /pharmacodynamic (PD) models. PK library includes model different administration routes (bolus, infusion, first-order absorption), different number compartments (1 3), different types eliminations (linear Michaelis-Menten). PD model library, contains direct immediate models (e.g. Emax Imax) various baseline models, turnover response models. PK/PD models obtained combination models PK PD model libraries. **PFIM** handles analytical ODE models offers possibility user define /model(s). **PFIM 7.0**, FIM evaluated first order linearization model assuming block diagonal FIM Mentré et al. (1997). Bayesian FIM also available give shrinkage predictions (Combes et al., 2013). **PFIM 7.0** includes several algorithms conduct design optimization based D-criterion, given design constraints: simplex algorithm (Nelder-Mead) (Nelder & Mead, 1965), multiplicative algorithm (Seurat et al., 2021), Fedorov-Wynn algorithm (Fedorov, 1972), PSO (*Particle Swarm Optimization*) PGBO (*Population Genetics Based Optimizer*) (Le Nagard et al., 2011).

title: Fisher Information matrix for design evaluation/optimization for nonlinear mixed effects models. — PFIM-package
text: **PFIM 7.0** also provides quality control tests validation using evaluated FIM assess validity new version new features. Finally, **PFIM 7.0** displays results clear graphical form data summary, ensuring easy manipulation R. standard data visualization package ggplot2 R used display results clear graphical form (Wickham, 2016). quality control using D-criterion also provided.

title: PSOAlgorithm Class — PSOAlgorithm
text: maxIteration integer specifying maximum number iterations algorithm stops. populationSize integer specifying number particles swarm. TRUE, algorithm prints current best fitness iteration progress R console. optimisationDesign list storing evaluations (FIM, criteria, SE) initial optimal design. optimisationAlgorithmOutputs list containing logs algorithm-specific outputs produced optimization process. name string representing name project evaluation study. modelParameters list defining fixed effects random effects (variances) model. modelEquations list containing mathematical equations model. modelFromLibrary list specifying pre-defined model selected PFIM library. modelError list specifying residual error model (e.g., constant, proportional, combined). designs list `Design` objects representing experimental protocols evaluated. outputs list defining observation variables responses model. fimType string specifying FIM calculation method (e.g., "Population", "Individual", "Bayesian"). odeSolverParameters list containing technical settings ODE solver, atol rtol.
